materials: Yarn in green (about 2m/2.2yd, shown in Caron Simply Soft Kelly Green) and red (34-40cm/12-16”, shown in Red Heart Soft Really Red)- any weight including thread will work, it will only influence the finished size
Hook matching your chosen yarn weight (I suggest 3.5mm/E for worsted yarn)
Optional: clear nail polish or white glue

rounds.rawInstruction: with green, make a slip knot and ch 14; sl st in the first ch you made, to join the piece in a circle; [ch 3, sl st in the next ch of the circle] 14 times (work your last sl st into the st that joined the circle) (14 chain-loops made)
rounds.summary: Create a foundation chain, join into a circle, and work 14 chain-3 loops around.
rounds.title: Round 1

rounds.rawInstruction: Fasten off and weave in the ends (or keep them for sewing, if the wreath will become an applique).
rounds.title: Fasten off and weave in the ends (or keep them for sewing, if the wreath will become an applique).

rounds.rawInstruction: Take a 30-40cm/12-16" length of red yarn, thread it into a yarn needle and weave it through the chain loops around the wreath, starting and ending at the join. Tie the ends in a bow, then cut them to the length you like.
rounds.summary: Thread red yarn through chain loops and tie in a bow.
rounds.title: Take a 30-40cm/12-16" length of red yarn, thread it into a yarn needle and weave it through the chain loops around the wreath, starting and ending at the join. Tie the ends in a bow, then cut them to the length you like.

rounds.rawInstruction: Optionally, if your yarn holds knots poorly or tends to fray at the ends, dot some clear nail polish or white glue at the knot and at the ends, to secure them.
rounds.summary: Apply glue or nail polish to secure knots and ends.
rounds.title: Optionally, if your yarn holds knots poorly or tends to fray at the ends, dot some clear nail polish or white glue at the knot and at the ends, to secure them.
